howItWorks.intro: This import tariff calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Additional Tariff = Goods Value × Additional Rate%
Landed Cost = CIF + All Duties + MPF + HMF
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is CIF value?
faq.answer: CIF (Cost, Insurance, and Freight) is the customs value used to calculate duties. It includes the goods value (FOB), shipping/freight costs, and insurance. Most countries base duties on CIF value.

faq.question: What are Section 301 tariffs?
faq.answer: Section 301 tariffs are additional duties imposed by the US on specific goods from countries found to have unfair trade practices. Currently, many Chinese goods face 7.5-25% additional tariffs on top of regular duties.

faq.question: What is the Merchandise Processing Fee?
faq.answer: The MPF is a US Customs fee of 0.3464% of the CIF value, with a minimum of $31.67 and maximum of $614.35 per entry. It applies to most formal entries.
